Title: FIONA1 is an RNA 6-methyladenosine N-methyltransferase affecting Arabidopsis photomorphogenesis and flowering

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: U6 snRNA and a small subset of poly(A)+ RNA, especially 3' UTR/stop-codon-proximal transcript regions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Post-transcriptional regulation affecting mRNA stability, flowering transition, and phytochrome signaling-dependent photomorphogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: U6 snRNA methylation and subset poly(A)+ RNA methylation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: METTL16-like RNA methyltransferase; conserved NPPF catalytic motif
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed in diverse tissues, especially high in buds and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed in diverse tissues, especially high in floral organs including buds and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native FIONA1 promoter (complementation); 35S promoter used for GFP construct
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Adenosine residues in U6 snRNA UACm6AGAGAA motif and non-U6 mRNA motifs such as UGUAU/UGUAG and GGACC
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: m6A-modified U6 snRNA and m6A-modified poly(A)+ RNAs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to the N6 position of adenosine in U6 snRNA and a subset of mRNAs
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for a small subset of Arabidopsis poly(A)+ RNA m6A sites; not established as globally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes m6A installation on U6 snRNA and selected mRNAs; loss of function reduces nuclear RNA/U6/poly(A)+ RNA m6A, causes early flowering and phytochrome signaling-dependent hypocotyl elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; native-promoter complementation with wild type versus catalytic-dead FIONA1m; LC-MS/MS; SELECT; in vitro methylation assay with recombinant protein; m6A-seq; m6A-IP-qPCR; RIP-qPCR; mRNA stability assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day and short-day photoperiods; continuous red and far-red light; high SAM or L-methionine treatments
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PIF4; CRY2; CO; FLC (direct RNA binding targets by RIP-qPCR); possible unidentified helper proteins for mRNA methylation in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytically inactive mutant FIONA1m carries P237A/F239G substitutions in conserved NPPF motif and fails to rescue methylation or phenotypes. FIONA1 does not methylate MAT1-4 SAM synthetase transcripts and does not alter SAM homeostasis under normal or high SAM conditions. FIONA1-dependent m6A sites are largely distinct from FIP37-dependent sites.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq and RNA-seq; raw sequencing data deposited in GSA CRA004052; comparative published FIP37-related dataset GSE75508
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes developmental defects including early flowering and elongated hypocotyls; no major viability defect reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate flowering time and light-responsive growth via epitranscriptome engineering, but pleiotropic developmental effects warrant caution
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FIONA1 is an Arabidopsis METTL16-like RNA methyltransferase that deposits m6A on U6 snRNA and a small subset of poly(A)+ RNAs. 2. Its catalytic activity is required for normal flowering and phytochrome signaling-dependent photomorphogenesis. 3. FIONA1 directly methylates non-U6 mRNA motifs in phenotypically relevant transcripts and alters their mRNA stability. 4. Unlike mammalian METTL16/worm METT-10, it does not regulate SAM synthetase transcripts or SAM homeostasis.
